summary: ANDROID ⛔ HSFWCH-9500 🟥 INTERMITTENT | TERN | EMU | OSPREY 🦅 Tapping on button on earbud during fit test will cause it to fail
scenario: Fit Test fails inconsistently when tapping earbud buttons on supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, Osprey)
description_clean: During a Fit Test on supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, Osprey), tapping a button on an earbud causes the fit test to fail or behave inconsistently. If the primary earbud button is pressed, the test shows a "device disconnected" failure message while the other earbud continues playing test audio. If the non-primary earbud button is pressed, the test lingers and eventually shows a message that the test cannot be completed at this time. Expected: button taps should not interfere with the fit test, or the app should handle the interruption gracefully and consistently.
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android
Connect supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, or Osprey) to the device
Tap on the connected device name to open Device Settings
Select Fit Test option to begin the ear tip fit test
During the fit test, tap the button on the primary earbud
Observe the behavior on screen and audio output
Repeat steps 1-6, but this time during the fit test, tap the button on the non-primary earbud
Observe the behavior on screen and audio output
expected: Tapping any button on either earbud during a Fit Test should not interfere with the test. The test should continue normally and complete successfully, or the app should gracefully handle the interruption with a consistent and clear message.
actual: When the primary earbud button is pressed during the Fit Test, the test shows a \"device disconnected\" failure message while the other earbud continues playing test audio. When the non-primary earbud button is pressed, the test lingers and eventually shows a message that the test cannot be completed at this time.
